name: Cortex v2.1 — Phone-Optimized Orb + Streaming & Duplicate-Send Fixes
body:
## What's fixed in v2.1

### Streaming reliability
- **Long replies no longer break** — auto-scroll now only fires when you're already pinned to the bottom, so reading older messages mid-stream isn't yanked away. Previously every token triggered an animated scroll that fought the renderer.
- **No more end-of-reply flicker** — the streaming bubble stays visible until the final saved message actually lands in the list, eliminating the brief content gap that happened when the bubble was cleared before the message arrived.

### Duplicate message fix
- **Double-tap / IME+button no longer sends twice** — `send()` now uses an atomic `compareAndSet` guard that closes the race window that previously allowed two user messages to be inserted. Same guard protects `regenerate()`.

### Orb animations finally smooth on phone
- The orb is now drawn in a **single Canvas pass** + `graphicsLayer` (reads animated state *inside* the layer block, so frames only invalidate the GPU layer, never recompose the composable). Previously each orb used nested `Box` composables with `Modifier.scale()`/`rotate()` that recomposed every frame — with many orbs in a chat list this meant jank on mid-range phones. Now it's buttery.
- Added an inner radial highlight + orbiting-dot glow for a glassier 3D feel.

### Cleaner, more comfortable UI
- **Send button moved outside the text field** — it's now a large 48dp circular button sitting next to the field (gradient when active, red when streaming). The old in-field trailing icon was cramped and easy to miss on phones.
- Haptic feedback on send.
- Stop button is the same circle, toggled red.

### Regenerate reworked
- `regenerate()` is now self-contained with the atomic guard — no more fragile `inputText` round-trip that could race with the message observer.

---

**Install:** download `cortex-v2.1-release.apk` below, open it on your Android device (enable "Install from unknown sources" if asked), tap Install. Your existing chats and gateways are preserved.

Min: Android 8.0 (API 26) · Target: Android 14 (API 34) · 12 MB
